tool.name: Filter empty datasets

param.name: replacement
param.label: Replace empty elements with this dataset
param.help: If provided, empty elements will be replaced with this dataset

tool.help:
======== Synopsis ========
Removes empty (zero-byte) datasets from a collection.
=========== Description ===========
This tool takes a dataset collection and filters out elements that have no content. A dataset is considered empty if it contains zero bytes of actual data (including compressed archives that decompress to nothing).
This is useful when a multi-sample analysis produces some empty outputs — for example, when a filtering step removes all reads for certain samples. Downstream tools often fail on empty inputs, so removing them lets the rest of the analysis proceed.
Optionally, you can provide a **replacement dataset**. When specified, empty elements are replaced with that dataset instead of being removed. This preserves the collection size, which is important when element-wise pairing with another collection is needed.
======== Examples ========
**Remove empty elements**
Given a list collection where one sample produced no output::
List: "Filtered reads"
sample1 → reads.fastq (1.2 MB) sample2 → reads.fastq (0 bytes) sample3 → reads.fastq (800 KB)
The output keeps only non-empty elements::
List: "Filtered reads (filtered empty datasets)"
sample1 → reads.fastq (1.2 MB) sample3 → reads.fastq (800 KB)
-------
**Replace instead of remove**
If a replacement dataset is provided, empty elements are swapped rather than dropped, preserving the collection structure::
List: "Filtered reads (filtered empty datasets)"
sample1 → reads.fastq (1.2 MB) sample2 → replacement.fastq sample3 → reads.fastq (800 KB)
-------
.. image:: ${static_path}/images/tools/collection_ops/filter_empty.svg :alt: Filter empty datasets from collection :width: 500
-------
.. class:: infomark
@QUOTA_USAGE_NOTE@
